column1: Source Table 2 row labels are preserved here. Runtime implementation should normalize merged duration spans into executable operation groups and use the explicit pair map; row order is not a pairing rule.

column7: Use explicit pair map; e.g., EQI-BD pairs with EQI-PR, while AD and BD are external/single operations.
AD&PP->BF and PP->PU are direct transfer rules; long/merged duration spans must be normalized before execution.
Do not infer partners by row order; pair validation must use normalized operation groups.
Normalize source table spans; direct pair map decides whether the displayed labels are simultaneous in an executable group.
Stage labels remain distinct even if native solver receives generic paired behavior.
Single/external operations and internal transfers are ledgered under separate categories.
The manifest validator must resolve overlapping spans into direct-pair groups.
Terminal states write back to the named beds shown here, not local bed indices.
PP->PU transfers are internal; AD product is external unless assigned to BF in the normalized group.
AD&PP->BF internal flow is separated from external product in the ledger.
